data.title: Autolus Therapeutics Balances Growth Ambition With Rising Losses
data.description:
Autolus Therapeutics ((AUTL)) has held its Q4 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Autolus Therapeutics’ latest earnings call painted a cautiously optimistic picture for investors. Management highlighted strong commercial momentum for AUCATZYL, promising real‑world data and a deep pipeline, but this came alongside sharply higher costs, a widened net loss and a nearly halved cash balance that put execution risk firmly in focus.

AUCATZYL’s first full year delivers solid revenue base

AUCATZYL’s first full commercial year generated $74.3 million in net product revenue, anchoring Autolus’s transition into a commercial-stage biotech. Fourth-quarter net product revenue reached $23.3 million, lifting total Q4 revenue to $24.3 million including a $1 million license milestone.

ROCCA real-world data confirm strong efficacy profile

The ROCCA consortium data were a key highlight, with 96 patients apheresed and 91 infused, yielding a 94.8% infusion rate that speaks to operational feasibility. Among 84 evaluable patients at day 28, overall complete remission was about 92%, mirroring FELIX trial results despite the less controlled real-world setting.

Safety outcomes in practice compare favorably to trials

In real-world use, AUCATZYL’s safety profile tracked better than pivotal data, with cytokine release syndrome mostly low grade at 59% Grade 1–2 and no Grade 3 or higher cases. Immune effector cell–associated neurotoxicity syndrome rates were also manageable at 17% Grade 1–2 and 3% Grade 3, comparing well with higher high-grade rates in FELIX.

Regulatory wins lay groundwork for future expansion

Autolus secured regulatory approvals in both the European Union and the U.K., an important strategic step for a long-term global franchise. While management does not expect material revenue from continental Europe in 2026, these approvals create an option on future geographic expansion once reimbursement paths are clearer.

Building a wider commercial footprint in key markets

By the end of 2025, Autolus had activated 67 treatment centers, providing a solid base for patient access and revenue growth. The company aims to push that network to more than 80 centers by the end of 2026, an increase of roughly 19%, which should help drive higher treatment volumes.

Pipeline advances target oncology and autoimmune markets

Autolus underscored its broader pipeline, led by CATULUS in pediatric ALL, which moves into Phase 2 with around 30 additional patients after Phase 1 showed about 95% CR/CRi and 91% CR, with pivotal data targeted by 2027. Autoimmune programs including CARLYSLE in advanced SLE, LUMINA in lupus nephritis, BOBCAT in progressive MS and ALARIC in AL amyloidosis offer multi-year upside but come with data timing and interpretability risks.

Path to positive gross margins hinges on scale

Management reiterated expectations to achieve positive gross margins in 2026, driven by higher patient volumes and better utilization of manufacturing infrastructure. The strategy centers on scaling throughput and improving operating efficiencies to spread fixed costs over a larger revenue base.

Cash runway extended but balance sharply lower

The company closed 2025 with $300.7 million in cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities, down from $588 million a year earlier, reflecting launch investments and R&D spend. Autolus believes this balance, despite being nearly 49% lower year over year, should fund operations into the fourth quarter of 2027.

Accounting shift tightens revenue and cost alignment

Autolus refined its accounting to recognize full product revenue and associated cost of goods sold only after confirmation of a patient’s second dose. This change removes earlier partial deferrals and better aligns revenue and cost timing, though it can delay revenue recognition for patients who do not receive both doses.

Net loss surges as commercialization costs bite

The fourth quarter showcased the cost of scaling, with net loss ballooning to $90.3 million from $27.6 million a year earlier, a roughly 227% increase. Management pointed to commercialization expenses and other launch-related items as the main drivers of this widened loss.

Higher cost of sales keeps margins in the red

Cost of sales more than doubled to $25.3 million in Q4 2025 from $11.4 million a year prior, slightly exceeding total revenue of $24.3 million and resulting in a negative gross margin. This underlines the urgency of Autolus’s 2026 margin improvement plan centered on volume growth and efficiency gains.

Operating expenses climb with R&D and SG&A growth

Research and development spending rose to $35.6 million in the quarter from $30.8 million, reflecting continued investment in the pipeline. Selling, general and administrative expenses also increased to $35.8 million from $33.7 million as the company built out its commercial organization and supporting infrastructure.

EU revenue delayed amid market access uncertainty

Despite securing EU approval, Autolus is not forecasting meaningful revenue from other European countries in 2026 as it evaluates reimbursement strategies. This delays international diversification and heightens reliance on the U.S. and U.K. markets to deliver the near-term growth needed to support the cost base.

Revenue timing nuances add short-term variability

Because revenue is now recognized upon the second dose, patients who receive only a single infusion can create timing mismatches depending on reimbursement specifics. This could introduce short-term volatility or deferrals in reported revenue even if underlying demand remains healthy.

Early-stage programs offer upside with execution risk

Several marquee programs, including BOBCAT for progressive MS, LUMINA in lupus nephritis and ALARIC in AL amyloidosis, are early stage or rely on single-arm designs, making future data harder to interpret. With pivotal readouts largely clustered in 2027–2028, investors face a multi-year wait and the inherent risk that early signals may not translate into commercial success.

Guidance underscores growth ambitions and margin inflection

Looking ahead, Autolus reaffirmed 2026 AUCATZYL net revenue guidance of $120 million to $135 million, implying roughly 62% to 82% growth over 2025. Management expects to turn gross margins positive next year as volumes rise, expand its network beyond 80 centers while relying mainly on U.S. and U.K. sales and operate with its current cash into late 2027.

Autolus’s earnings call outlined a classic high-risk, high-reward biotech transition story, with strong launch trends, compelling real-world data and a broad pipeline set against heavy spending and a steep cash drawdown. For equity investors, the setup hinges on whether AUCATZYL can scale fast enough and whether the pipeline can deliver pivotal readouts that justify today’s investment in growth.

The latest update is out from Labcorp Holdings ( (LH) ).

Labcorp Holdings Inc., a major global laboratory services provider, plans to release its financial results for the first quarter of 2026 before the U.S. market opens on Thursday, April 30, 2026, and will host a webcast at 9:00 a.m. ET that day to discuss the numbers. The company will post the earnings release and detailed financial materials, along with a replay of the webcast available for one year, on its investor relations website, signaling transparency and continued engagement with investors around its performance and outlook.

The announcement, issued from Burlington, N.C., on March 31, 2026, underscores Labcorp’s focus on timely communication of quarterly results as it operates at significant scale, having supported a majority of recent FDA drug approvals and conducted hundreds of millions of tests globally. Investors and industry stakeholders will scrutinize the upcoming first-quarter disclosure for insight into demand trends in diagnostics and drug development services, as well as Labcorp’s positioning amid ongoing healthcare and biopharma activity.

Through its scale and scientific expertise, Labcorp plays a central role in both routine and specialized diagnostics as well as clinical development for new therapies, positioning it as a key infrastructure provider in the healthcare and life sciences ecosystem. Its broad client base and global operational footprint make its financial performance and strategic direction closely watched by healthcare stakeholders and investors.

data.title: Chegg Rebalances Board Structure, Revises Director Class Terms
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Chegg ( (CHGG) ).

On March 25, 2026, Chegg’s board rebalanced its staggered director classes by shifting director Renee Budig from Class I, which was set to expire at the June 12, 2026 annual meeting, to Class III with a term extending to the 2028 annual meeting. To implement this, Budig briefly resigned and was immediately re-elected as a Class III director, with her board service treated as uninterrupted.

Following the rebalance, Chegg’s board now consists of two Class I directors, one Class II director, and two Class III directors, creating a more even distribution of terms. The company also structured the change so that shareholders will still have the chance to vote on Budig’s continued board membership at the 2026 annual meeting, helping maintain regular investor oversight and governance continuity.

The most recent analyst rating on (CHGG) stock is a Hold with a $0.66 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Chegg stock, see the CHGG Stock Forecast page.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (declining revenue, ongoing losses, and negative free cash flow) and bearish technicals (below key moving averages with negative MACD). Positives from the earnings call—cost cuts, EBITDA guidance, and Skilling momentum—provide some offset, but near-term cash needs and listing risk keep the overall profile low.

data.title: Barfresh Sets Strong 2026 Growth Outlook and Financing
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Barfresh Food Group ( (BRFH) ) just unveiled an update.

Barfresh reported that fiscal 2025 was a transformational year, posting record annual revenue of $14.2 million, up 33% from 2024, and fourth-quarter revenue of $5.4 million, up 94% year over year, largely driven by the Arps Dairy acquisition and expanded school and Pop & Go sales. However, margins compressed as gross margin fell to 22% for 2025 and 3% in the fourth quarter due to startup and relocation costs tied to shifting production in-house, while full-year net loss narrowed slightly to $2.7 million and adjusted EBITDA loss widened to $2.1 million as the company invested in its new facility and integrated Arps’ lower-margin milk business.

Looking ahead, the company on March 31, 2026, updated its outlook, guiding first-quarter 2026 revenue to $5.0–$5.2 million, implying up to 77% growth, and setting full-year 2026 revenue guidance at $28–$32 million with adjusted EBITDA of $3.2–$3.8 million, reflecting a more conservative ramp-up of its upgraded plant. Barfresh also recently secured $7.5 million in strategic financing to fully own and expand its manufacturing platform, which management says can support more than $200 million in annual revenue, positioning the group for significant scale and potentially improved margins once transition costs subside.

The most recent analyst rating on (BRFH) stock is a Hold with a $3.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Barfresh Food Group stock, see the BRFH Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on BRFH Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, BRFH is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial performance (ongoing losses, continued cash burn, and a more leveraged balance sheet). Offsetting this, management commentary and guidance are strong, supported by record revenue and positive adjusted EBITDA, while technicals are neutral-to-slightly positive. Valuation remains a caution due to negative earnings and no dividend signal.

To see Spark’s full report on BRFH stock, click here.

More about Barfresh Food Group

Barfresh Food Group Inc., based in Los Angeles and listed on Nasdaq as BRFH, provides frozen, ready-to-blend and ready-to-drink beverages. The company increasingly manufactures in-house, aided by its acquisition of Arps Dairy and a 44,000-square-foot facility that also processes milk, supporting both its beverage production and supply diversification, with a growing focus on the education channel.

data.title: Udemy Issues Supplemental Disclosures on Coursera Merger Deal
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Udemy Inc ( (UDMY) ) has shared an update.

On December 17, 2025, Udemy agreed to merge with Coursera in an all-stock transaction that would make Udemy a wholly owned subsidiary of Coursera, and a related registration statement and joint proxy statement/prospectus became effective on March 10, 2026 ahead of both companies’ April 9, 2026 special stockholder meetings to vote on the deal. Following the merger announcement, three lawsuits and multiple stockholder demand letters challenged the adequacy of disclosures in the joint proxy statement/prospectus, prompting Udemy and Coursera—while denying any wrongdoing—to issue detailed supplemental disclosures on board deliberations, valuation assumptions, financial projections and comparable analyses to reduce legal friction and avoid delays to closing the transaction.

The supplemental disclosures expand on Coursera’s M&A committee process, Udemy board discussions of alternative bidders, the proposed exchange ratio and pro forma ownership, as well as Morgan Stanley’s and Qatalyst’s valuation work using cash levels, revenue multiples, discounted cash flow analyses, precedent transaction premia and analyst price targets for both companies. By voluntarily enhancing transparency around the financial and strategic rationale for the merger, the companies aim to mitigate disclosure-based challenges, reassure investors about the fairness of terms and preserve momentum toward combining two major players in the online education sector.

The most recent analyst rating on (UDMY) stock is a Hold with a $4.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Udemy Inc stock, see the UDMY Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on UDMY Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, UDMY is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by improving financial quality (stronger margins, solid positive free cash flow, and low leverage). Offsetting this are weak technical trends and an expensive earnings multiple (high P/E, no dividend support). The Coursera merger-related developments provide a positive, but secondary, catalyst.

To see Spark’s full report on UDMY stock, click here.

More about Udemy Inc

Udemy, Inc. operates in the digital education industry, offering an online learning platform that connects instructors with learners globally across consumer and enterprise segments. The company focuses on technology-enabled course delivery and upskilling content, competing with other e-learning providers as it targets both individual learners and corporate training markets.

An announcement from BriaCell Therapeutics ( (TSE:BCT) ) is now available.

On March 31, 2026, BriaPro Therapeutics filed unaudited condensed interim consolidated financial statements and management’s discussion and analysis for the three- and six-month periods ended January 31, 2026 with securities regulators in British Columbia and Alberta, covering activity in 2025 and 2026. The filings show a small asset base of about $176,890 against current and non-current liabilities totaling roughly $1.8 million, a shareholders’ deficit exceeding $1.6 million, and rising research and development costs that drove comprehensive losses of $227,493 for the quarter and $472,231 for the six months, signaling ongoing funding needs and balance-sheet pressure for stakeholders.

The interim results highlight that BriaPro’s cash position is minimal, its deficit has widened from $1.15 million in July 2025 to $1.62 million by January 31, 2026, and amounts due to related parties have increased to more than $1.6 million. With R&D spending more than doubling year over year and a warrant liability that adds further complexity to the capital structure, the company’s dependence on external financing and related-party support remains a key risk factor for investors and creditors.

data.title: Vestand Inc. Delays 2025 Annual SEC Filing
data.description:
Vestand Inc Class A ( (VSTD) ) has released a notification of late filing.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Vestand Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25, notifying investors that it will not meet the deadline for its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the financial year ended December 31, 2025. The delayed filing relates to the company’s Annual Report and follows earlier disclosures that prior financial statements from late 2022 through mid-2025 can no longer be relied upon.

The primary reason for the delay is an ongoing accounting restatement process. The board, acting on an audit committee report and an independent accountant’s agreed-upon procedures review, concluded that previously issued financial statements contained accounting errors and inaccuracies significant enough to require correction.

Vestand plans to restate all affected historical financial statements and to correct any additional errors identified during the review. Because this restatement work is still in progress, management states that it cannot complete the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) within the original timetable without incurring unreasonable effort or expense.

The company has not committed to a specific date for when the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) will be filed. Instead, it indicates that the annual report will be submitted to the SEC “as soon as practicable” after the restatement process is finished, signaling that investors may face continued uncertainty over timing.

Vestand also confirms that its Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q (Quarter Report) for the period ended September 30, 2025, remains outstanding. This adds to the company’s backlog of delayed SEC reports and underscores the depth of the financial reporting issues tied to the restatement.

Management warns that the restatement is expected to have a financial impact on results for the year ended December 31, 2025. However, the company says it is not yet able to reasonably estimate the magnitude or direction of those changes, leaving investors without quantitative guidance while the review continues.

The notification includes customary forward-looking statement language, emphasizing that expectations around restatement timing, future SEC filings, and financial outcomes are subject to risks and uncertainties. Vestand cautions that further accounting errors could be uncovered, and that actual results may differ materially from current expectations.

To demonstrate ongoing compliance efforts, the company explains that it is working through the restatement process and intends to complete and file its overdue reports once the review is finalized. The notification is signed by Chief Executive Officer Jiwon Kim on March 31, 2026, formally confirming executive-level oversight of the late-filing process.

The most recent analyst rating on (VSTD) stock is a Hold with a $0.30 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Vestand Inc Class A stock, see the VSTD Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on VSTD Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, VSTD is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial health (ongoing losses, negative margins, and high leverage) and significant negative corporate events tied to Nasdaq compliance and litigation risk. Technicals show only a tentative short-term bounce within a broader downtrend, and valuation is not supportive given negative earnings and no dividend.

To see Spark’s full report on VSTD stock, click here.

More about Vestand Inc Class A

Vestand Inc. Class A, formerly known as Yoshiharu Global Co., is a U.S.-based company headquartered in Brea, California. The filing does not provide details on its industry, operations, or primary business activities, focusing instead on regulatory and financial reporting matters related to its SEC filings.

Average Trading Volume: 129,576

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

data.description:
IIOT-OXYS ( (ITOX) ) has released a notification of late filing.

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

IIOT-OXYS, Inc. has notified investors that it will delay filing its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025, via a Form 12b-25 with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. The filing extension centers on the company’s inability to finalize its annual report review with its independent auditor in time for the original deadline.

The company states that the late filing stems specifically from not yet completing the review process with the auditor, rather than from any disclosed internal control breakdowns, restatements, or governance crises. There is no mention of auditor changes or special committee reviews, suggesting the issue is mainly timing and completion of audit procedures.

IIOT-OXYS, Inc. has indicated that it expects to file the delayed Form 10-K (Yearly Report) within the standard SEC grace period. The company has committed to submitting the annual report no later than the fifteenth calendar day following the original prescribed due date, which should limit any prolonged uncertainty for shareholders.

From a financial impact perspective, the company does not anticipate any significant change in results of operations compared with the prior fiscal year, based on what it has disclosed in the notification. Investors should keep in mind that these statements about expected results and timing are effectively forward-looking and remain subject to completion of the audit and final review.

IIOT-OXYS, Inc. emphasizes that all other required periodic reports over the past 12 months have been filed on time, underscoring an effort to remain broadly compliant with SEC reporting obligations. The notification was signed on March 31, 2026, by Chief Executive Officer Clifford L. Emmons, signaling executive-level oversight of the delayed filing and the company’s ongoing push to complete and file its annual report promptly.

data.title: Bespoke Extracts Delays Annual 10-K Filing
data.description:
Bespoke Extracts ( (BSPK) ) has released a notification of late filing.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Bespoke Extracts, Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25 to notify investors of a delay in submitting its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the financial year ended December 31, 2025. The filing concerns the company’s annual report and confirms that all other required periodic reports over the past 12 months have been filed on time.

The company attributes the delay primarily to the additional time needed to complete the compilation, review, and finalization of its financial statements and related disclosures for the Form 10-K (Yearly Report). Management cites ongoing coordination with its independent registered public accounting firm and internal reviews by both management and the audit committee as key factors.

Bespoke Extracts indicates that these procedures imposed time constraints that could not be resolved without unreasonable effort or expense. However, it emphasizes that the delay is procedural rather than the result of a disclosed control failure or a change in auditors, and that work on the report is actively underway.

The company states that it expects to file its delayed Form 10-K (Yearly Report) no later than fifteen calendar days after the original filing deadline. This timeline aligns with the extension allowed under SEC Rule 12b-25 for late annual reports, suggesting the company aims to remain within the grace period and avoid falling out of reporting compliance.

From a financial perspective, Bespoke Extracts discloses that revenue for the year ended December 31, 2025, is estimated to have grown to approximately $1,501,000 from $1,117,452 in 2024. At the same time, the company is unable to provide a reasonable estimate of its net loss for 2025, highlighting some uncertainty around bottom-line performance.

The lack of a net loss estimate means investors should treat these disclosures as forward-looking and subject to change once the audited Form 10-K (Yearly Report) is filed. The revenue increase is positive on its face, but the final report will be crucial to understanding margins, expenses, and overall financial health.

In terms of next steps, Bespoke Extracts underscores that its management and audit committee are continuing their internal reviews while working closely with the independent auditor to finalize the accounts. The notification is signed on behalf of the company by Chief Executive Officer Michael Feinsod, dated March 31, 2026, signaling executive-level responsibility for the delay and the commitment to complete the filing promptly.

More about Bespoke Extracts

Bespoke Extracts, Inc. is a U.S.-based company operating in the consumer products space, producing and selling specialty extract-based products. The company is publicly traded and files regular reports with the Securities and Exchange Commission as an Exchange Act registrant. Its principal executive office is located in Aurora, Colorado.

MediXall Group, Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25 to notify investors that it will be late in submitting its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the financial year ended December 31, 2025. The delayed filing concerns the company’s annual report with the U.S. securities regulator.

As with all such notices, these expectations are forward-looking and could differ from the final audited numbers once the reports are completed.

MediXall Group says it is actively preparing the outstanding annual and quarterly reports to bring its SEC filings up to date and return to full compliance. data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

CaliberCos, Inc. Class A ( (CWD) ) has shared an announcement.

On March 30, 2026, CaliberCos Inc. announced that an institutional investor had converted approximately $15.9 million of perpetual, non-dividend-paying Series B preferred equity into 63,472 shares of common stock at a conversion price of $250 per share. The move, previously disclosed in the company’s March 25, 2026 annual report filing, removes a significant layer of preferred equity from Caliber’s capital structure, leaving more of the capital stack in common equity and thereby reducing securities senior to common shareholders while simplifying the firm’s overall financing profile.

By replacing preferred equity with common shares, Caliber shifts risk and potential upside more squarely onto its common equity base, which can have implications for existing shareholders through dilution but also clarifies priority in the capital stack. The streamlined structure may enhance the company’s financial flexibility and capital markets profile as it continues to pursue its strategy in middle-market real estate and digital asset-enabled investment platforms.
